---
title: "Data Visualisation with Python"
description: "An interactive line chart using Plotly and Pandas."
author: "Aidan Mistry"
date: "2026-04-23"
categories: [Python, Data Analysis, Plotly]
---

## Introduction

This project demonstrates my ability to embed Python code directly into a Quarto document, generating interactive data visualisations.

## Interactive Plot

Below is a Python code chunk that generates an interactive line chart. You can hover over the data points to see the exact values!

```{python}
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Create a sample dataset
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Day": ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri"],
    "Productivity Level": [70, 85, 90, 65, 95]
})

# Generate the interactive plot
fig = px.line(
    df, 
    x="Day", 
    y="Productivity Level", 
    title="Weekly Productivity Trend", 
    markers=True
)
fig.show()
```
